title: Why BigBear.ai's Impressive Backlog Comes With a Big Asterisk
text: BigBear.ai's $385 million backlog sounds impressive, but analysis reveals significant caveats that investors should understand. The vast majority of BigBear's backlog consists of unreliable revenue sources. Unexercised options: Nearly 77% of the total backlog ($295 million combined) represents optional contract extensions that customers may never activate. Unfunded contracts: Another substantial portion lacks appropriated funding. Funded backlog: Only 5% represents truly secured, funded contracts. Unexercised options relate to the company's existing contracts if our customer elects to exercise all of the options available in the contract. The concerning part is that despite this large backlog, BigBear's actual financial performance remains lackluster. 2024 revenue grew just 2% to $158 million. 2023 revenue was essentially flat. The company posted $230 million in losses over trailing twelve months. Federal budget constraints could further limit backlog conversion. The Motley Fool recommends caution, suggesting investors hold off on BigBear until it demonstrates meaningful profitability and sustained growth—distinguishing it clearly from comparable companies like Palantir.
